AnalysisWarning.description:
Structured warning for migration analysis.

Provides detailed context about issues discovered during ASG extraction
that may affect the migration to Snowflake.

Example:
    AnalysisWarning(
        code="W001",
        severity=WarningSeverity.WARNING,
        node_id="tx_011",
        message="Missing source logic...",
        source_module="my_security_lib",
        suggested_action="Provide source code or create equivalent UDF"
    )

regex_evidence.description: Structured evidence from regex fallback parsing. Contains: match_type, raw_snippet, identified_elements, failure_reason, primary_parser (ast/sqlglot)
regex_evidence.title: Regex Evidence

AnalysisWarning.title: AnalysisWarning

BindingAction.description: Type of action in an execution binding.
BindingAction.title: BindingAction

BindingSourceType.description: Type of source for an input binding.
BindingSourceType.title: BindingSourceType

CallBindings.description: Input and output bindings for a function call.
CallBindings.title: CallBindings

CallLocation.description: Location where a function call occurs.
CallLocation.title: CallLocation

ColumnConstraint.description:
A constraint on a column extracted from filter/where conditions.

Useful for synthetic data generation to ensure test data satisfies
the constraints that would pass through filters.

source_transformation.description: ID of the transformation where constraint was found
source_transformation.title: Source Transformation

ColumnConstraint.title: ColumnConstraint

ColumnRelationship.description:
A relationship between columns across data sources.

Extracted from join conditions to understand how tables/files relate.
Useful for synthetic data generation to ensure referential integrity.
ColumnRelationship.title: ColumnRelationship

ConstraintType.description: Type of column constraint extracted from filter conditions.
ConstraintType.title: ConstraintType

ControlBranch.description:
A single branch within a control node.

For if/else: branches are [true_branch, false_branch]
For match/case: branches are [case_1, case_2, ..., default]
For try/except: branches are [try_block, except_block, finally_block]

label.description: Branch identifier (true, false, case_X, try_block, etc.)
label.title: Label

steps.description: List of step IDs or transformation IDs in this branch
steps.title: Steps

sub_controls.description: Nested control node IDs for complex structures
sub_controls.title: Sub Controls

produces_dataframe.description: Whether this branch produces a DataFrame output
produces_dataframe.title: Produces Dataframe

target_variable.description: Variable name assigned in this branch (for MERGE detection)
target_variable.title: Target Variable

ssa_output_id.description: SSA-compliant output ID for this branch. When target_variable is shared across branches (e.g., df_processed), each branch gets a unique ssa_output_id (e.g., tx_015_LATAM, tx_018_STD). This enables unambiguous lineage tracking.
ssa_output_id.title: Ssa Output Id

ControlBranch.title: ControlBranch

ControlLogic.description: The condition or iterator expression for a control node.

resolved_expression.description: Fully resolved expression including variable definitions. E.g., if expression is 'is_latam' and is_latam = config.get('region') == 'LATAM', resolved_expression would be the full expression.
resolved_expression.title: Resolved Expression

ControlLogic.title: ControlLogic

ControlNode.description:
Universal control flow node for the ASG-Spark.

This abstracts any Python control structure into a graph topology pattern,
enabling Phase 2/3 to understand control flow without parsing Python syntax.

Examples:
- if/elif/else → BRANCH with 2+ branches
- match/case → BRANCH with N case branches + default
- for/while → LOOP with body branch + condition
- try/except/finally → PROTECTED with try/except/finally branches
- with → SCOPED with body branch + context metadata

loop_type.description: Purpose of the loop (for translation decisions)

loop_iterable.description: What is being iterated (range, list, DataFrame)
loop_iterable.title: Loop Iterable

source_location.description: Where this control structure appears in source

affects_dataframe.description: Whether this control affects DataFrame lineage
affects_dataframe.title: Affects Dataframe

is_opaque.description: True if logic cannot be translated to SQL
is_opaque.title: Is Opaque

opaque_code.description: Structured code for why this block is opaque

is_unrollable.description: True if loop iterates over static iterable known at compile time
is_unrollable.title: Is Unrollable

static_iterable.description: The static values to unroll (e.g., ['a', 'b', 'c'] or [0, 1, 2])
static_iterable.title: Static Iterable

convergence_point.description: ID of the first node after this control block that consumes branch outputs. Only set when exit_strategy=MERGE and there is code after the control block.
convergence_point.title: Convergence Point

branch_outputs.description: IDs of the last transformation node in each branch. These are the potential inputs to the convergence_point.
branch_outputs.title: Branch Outputs

merge_semantic.description: How branch outputs should be merged at convergence_point. Values: 'UNION_ALL' (combine all rows), 'CONDITIONAL' (runtime decides), 'EXCLUSIVE' (only one branch executes). Default is 'CONDITIONAL' for if/else.
merge_semantic.title: Merge Semantic

type_reconciliation_required.description: True if branches produce columns with potentially different types. Phase 2 must force type reconciliation at convergence_point.
type_reconciliation_required.title: Type Reconciliation Required

type_warnings.description: List of type inconsistency warnings between branches, e.g., 'local_tax: LATAM branch has CAST, STD branch uses raw amount'
type_warnings.title: Type Warnings

reconciliation_strategy.description: How Phase 2 should handle type reconciliation. Values: 'INJECT_BEFORE_CONVERGENCE' (insert virtual ReconciliationStep before convergence_point), 'INLINE_AT_CONVERGENCE' (modify convergence_point directly). Default recommendation: INJECT_BEFORE_CONVERGENCE to maintain separation of concerns.
reconciliation_strategy.title: Reconciliation Strategy

ControlNode.title: ControlNode

ControlType.description:
Universal control flow patterns.

These abstract Python's control structures into graph topology patterns:
- BRANCH: Conditional paths (if/elif/else, match/case)
- LOOP: Iterative execution (for, while)
- PROTECTED: Error handling (try/except/finally)
- SCOPED: Context management (with statement)
ControlType.title: ControlType

DataSink.description: A data sink (output) in the pipeline.

is_test_file.description: True if source is from a test file (path contains /tests/ or /test/)
is_test_file.title: Is Test File

is_empty_fallback.description: True if source is an empty DataFrame fallback (spark.createDataFrame([]))
is_empty_fallback.title: Is Empty Fallback

is_indirect.description: True if this output is written via a utility function (e.g., write_to_s3()) rather than a direct df.write call
is_indirect.title: Is Indirect

via_function.description: Name of the utility function that performs the actual write (e.g., 'data_update_into_s3'). Only set when is_indirect=True
via_function.title: Via Function

DataSink.title: DataSink

DataSource.description: A data source (input) in the pipeline.

required_columns.description: Columns required by downstream transformations with origin tracking
required_columns.title: Required Columns

is_test_file.description: True if source is from a test file (path contains /tests/ or /test/)
is_test_file.title: Is Test File

is_empty_fallback.description: True if source is an empty DataFrame fallback (spark.createDataFrame([]))
is_empty_fallback.title: Is Empty Fallback

is_indirect.description: True if this input is read via a utility function rather than a direct spark.read call
is_indirect.title: Is Indirect

via_function.description: Name of the utility function that performs the actual read. Only set when is_indirect=True
via_function.title: Via Function

DataSource.title: DataSource

ExecutionBinding.description:
A single binding action in an execution instance.

Either RESOLVE_INBOUND (captures literal -> data source) or
EXECUTE_FLOW (function call with parameter mappings).
ExecutionBinding.title: ExecutionBinding

ExecutionCall.description:
A function invocation with resolved bindings.

Captures the call graph: which function calls which, with what arguments,
and where the return value is stored. This enables tracing data flow
through function boundaries.

Example:
    silver_df = process_bronze_to_silver(df_sales, df_products)

Becomes:
    ExecutionCall(
        call_id="call_001",
        caller=CallLocation(function="run_pipeline", line=62, file="main.py"),
        callee=CalleeRef(function="process_bronze_to_silver"),
        bindings=CallBindings(
            inputs=[
                InputBinding(arg_name="df_sales", source_type="data_in", source_id="in_008"),
                InputBinding(arg_name="df_products", source_type="data_in", source_id="in_009"),
            ],
            output=OutputBinding(variable_name="silver_df", target_node="tx_004")
        )
    )

literal_arguments.description: Resolved string literals for arguments (e.g., table_name -> 'my_table')
literal_arguments.title: Literal Arguments

ExecutionCall.title: ExecutionCall

ExecutionInstance.description:
Represents a single execution flow from an entry point.

Captures how data flows from literals/sources through function calls,
enabling late binding resolution of inferred_schema_origin.

Example: When __main__ calls _read_table("sales_data") and then
passes the result to run_pipeline(), this tracks the full lineage.
ExecutionInstance.title: ExecutionInstance

ExitStrategy.description:
How control flow branches reunify after the control block.

- MERGE: All branches converge to a single output (common variable assignment)
- INDEPENDENT_SINK: Each branch writes to different destinations
- TERMINATE: Flow ends (raise, return, sys.exit)
ExitStrategy.title: ExitStrategy

ExtractionMetadata.description:
Metadata about the ASG extraction process and source context.

Groups project-level information separate from the graph structure.

workload_root.description: Absolute path to workload directory (base for relative paths)
workload_root.title: Workload Root

source_file.description: Path to input file/directory. Deprecated: use workload_root
source_file.title: Source File

generated_at.description: When the extraction was performed (ISO format)
generated_at.title: Generated At

ExtractionMetadata.title: ExtractionMetadata

FunctionArgument.description:
Represents a function argument with schema tracking capabilities.

Enables schema propagation through function calls by linking
arguments to their data sources.
FunctionArgument.title: FunctionArgument

FunctionDefinition.description:
Complete function definition with argument and return tracking.

Enables:
- Schema propagation through function arguments
- Lineage tracking from data sources to function parameters
- Return type inference for downstream consumers

is_test_file.description: True if source is from a test file (path contains /tests/ or /test/)
is_test_file.title: Is Test File

is_empty_fallback.description: True if source is an empty DataFrame fallback (spark.createDataFrame([]))
is_empty_fallback.title: Is Empty Fallback

is_udf.description: True if this function is registered or used as a Spark UDF
is_udf.title: Is Udf

FunctionDefinition.title: FunctionDefinition

FunctionReturn.description:
Represents the return value of a function.

Uses ref_type and ref_id to precisely identify what is returned:
- transformation: Points to the last tx_xxx node in a Spark chain
- variable: Points to a named variable (e.g., "df_final")
- multiple: Function has multiple return paths
- literal: Returns a constant value
- void: No return statement (action/sink functions)
FunctionReturn.title: FunctionReturn

ImportEntry.description:
Structured import entry with alias tracking.

Enables Name Resolution: when the lifter sees `F.col('x')`,
it can look up that `F` maps to `pyspark.sql.functions`.

The has_source field indicates whether the source code is available
in the workload directory. For CUSTOM_LIBRARY imports:
- has_source=True: We can analyze the implementation
- has_source=False: External dependency, cannot verify migration equivalence

has_source.description: Whether source code is available in the workload (relevant for custom_library)
has_source.title: Has Source

ImportEntry.title: ImportEntry

InferenceSummary.description: Summary of type inference results during transformation detection.

inferred.description: Transformations where type was successfully inferred
inferred.title: Inferred

excluded.description: Calls excluded because receiver is not DataFrame
excluded.title: Excluded

InferenceSummary.title: InferenceSummary

InferredColumn.description:
A column with inference metadata.

Used by the Schema Tracker to represent columns discovered
through code analysis rather than external catalog.

inferred_type.description: Inferred type. Can be single (STRING) or composite (STRING | INT)
inferred_type.title: Inferred Type

default_value.description: Default value when known (e.g., widget defaults, literal assignments)
default_value.title: Default Value

first_seen_nodes.description: Node IDs where first detected, ordered by earliest first
first_seen_nodes.title: First Seen Nodes

InferredColumn.title: InferredColumn

InferredColumnRef.description:
Column reference with origin tracking for data_in nodes.

Tracks which columns are required by downstream transformations
and why (e.g., join, filter, select).

source.description: Why this column is needed (join_requirement, filter_condition, select, aggregation)
source.title: Source

origin_node.description: The transformation node that requires this column (tx_004)
origin_node.title: Origin Node

InferredColumnRef.title: InferredColumnRef

InputBinding.description:
Maps a function argument to its source at call time.

Example: When calling `process_bronze_to_silver(df_sales, df_products)`,
this tracks that `df_sales` comes from `in_008` (a data source).

source_id.description: Source identifier (in_008, tx_004, call_001, or variable name)
source_id.title: Source Id

inferred_origin.description: Resolved data origin (e.g., 'data_in.sales_data') propagated through call graph
inferred_origin.title: Inferred Origin

InputBinding.title: InputBinding

InputColumn.description:
A column in a transformation's inferred_input.

Extends InferredColumn with a required from_inputs field that tracks
which input nodes this column originates from. For joins, the same
column may come from multiple inputs.

inferred_type.description: Inferred type. Can be single (STRING) or composite (STRING | INT)
inferred_type.title: Inferred Type

default_value.description: Default value when known (e.g., widget defaults, literal assignments)
default_value.title: Default Value

first_seen_nodes.description: Node IDs where first detected, ordered by earliest first
first_seen_nodes.title: First Seen Nodes

from_inputs.description: Input node IDs this column comes from (required, at least one)
from_inputs.title: From Inputs

InputColumn.title: InputColumn

InputMapping.description: Maps a function parameter to its origin node.
InputMapping.title: InputMapping

LoopType.description:
Distinguishes loop purposes for correct translation.

- CODE_GENERATION: Loop creates code artifacts (e.g., 12 columns for months)
- DATA_ITERATION: Loop processes data iteratively
- TABLE_ITERATION: Loop processes multiple tables/files
LoopType.title: LoopType

NotebookDependency.description: A dependency between Databricks notebooks via %run or dbutils.notebook.run.

target.description: Raw target path as written in %run (e.g., '../config', './utils')
target.title: Target

resolved_path.description: Resolved relative path from workload root (e.g., '.databricks/notebooks/rsuccess/config.py')
resolved_path.title: Resolved Path

line.description: Line number in source file where the dependency is declared
line.title: Line

NotebookDependency.title: NotebookDependency

OpaqueCode.description:
Structured codes for why a control block cannot be translated to SQL.

These codes enable Phase 2/3 to make automatic decisions:
- UNSUPPORTED_LIB → Try Python UDF
- IO_SIDE_EFFECT → Critical error, manual intervention
- COMPLEX_RECURSION → Stored Procedure
- DYNAMIC_SCHEMA → Warning + manual review
- EXTERNAL_API → Stored Procedure with External Access
- STATEFUL_ITERATION → UDF with state accumulator
- UNKNOWN → Generic opacity, requires analysis
OpaqueCode.title: OpaqueCode

OutputBinding.description:
Maps function return to variable and traceable node.

Example: `silver_df = process_bronze_to_silver(...)` binds the return
to variable `silver_df`, which resolves to transformation `tx_004`.

resolved_origin.description: Data origin this call produces (e.g., 'data_in.sales_data' for _read_table)
resolved_origin.title: Resolved Origin

OutputBinding.title: OutputBinding

ParsedFileInfo.description: Information about a parsed file with two-phase status.

syntax_correction.description: Description of automatic syntax correction applied
syntax_correction.title: Syntax Correction

syntax_error.description: Syntax error message if parsing failed
syntax_error.title: Syntax Error

understanding_error.description: Understanding error message if extraction failed
understanding_error.title: Understanding Error

ParsedFileInfo.title: ParsedFileInfo

ParsingReport.description:
Report of file processing results for a directory.

Three-phase processing:
1. Syntax Parsing: Validates Python syntax, auto-corrects where possible
2. Pipeline Understanding: Extracts semantic information about data transformations
3. Type Inference: Determines receiver types for accurate transformation detection

total_files.description: Total number of Python files found
total_files.title: Total Files

databricks_notebooks.description: Number of files that are Databricks notebook exports
databricks_notebooks.title: Databricks Notebooks

python_scripts.description: Number of pure Python script files
python_scripts.title: Python Scripts

inference.description: Type inference results for transformation detection

files.description: Details for files with corrections or errors
files.title: Files

inference_warnings.description: Warnings for calls resolved by name matching or excluded
inference_warnings.title: Inference Warnings

ParsingReport.title: ParsingReport

ReturnRefType.description: Type of reference for function return values.
ReturnRefType.title: ReturnRefType

SourceFile.description:
Represents a single source file with its imports.

Enables tracking of which imports belong to which file when
processing a multi-file workload. Functions are stored at the
ASG level with a source_file field pointing back to their origin.

Entrypoint detection fields enable downstream tools to identify
execution starting points without re-parsing source code.

source_type.description: Type of source file: NOTEBOOK, MODULE, SCRIPT

is_entry_point.description: True if this file is an execution entry point
is_entry_point.title: Is Entry Point

has_spark_session.description: True if this file creates a SparkSession
has_spark_session.title: Has Spark Session

notebook_dependencies.description: Other notebooks this file depends on via %run or dbutils.notebook.run
notebook_dependencies.title: Notebook Dependencies

widget_parameters.description: Deprecated: widgets are now promoted to data_in config entries
widget_parameters.title: Widget Parameters

description.description: Description extracted from the first %md section of the notebook
description.title: Description

pip_dependencies.description: Python packages installed via %pip in this notebook
pip_dependencies.title: Pip Dependencies

display_outputs.description: Number of display()/show() calls for notebook visualization
display_outputs.title: Display Outputs

SourceFile.title: SourceFile

SourceLocation.description:
Location of a code element in the source files.

Provides precise source tracking with:
- pathfile: Relative path from workload_root
- scope: Containing class/function hierarchy (e.g., "MyClass.my_method")
- span: Line and column range (e.g., "27:9-27:60")

scope.description: Containing class.function hierarchy, null if module-level
scope.title: Scope

SourceLocation.title: SourceLocation

SourceType.description: Type of source file (for entrypoint detection).
SourceType.title: SourceType

SparkCategory.description: Categories of Spark operations for classification.
SparkCategory.title: SparkCategory

corrected.description: Files with syntax errors that were auto-corrected
corrected.title: Corrected

errors.description: Files with syntax errors that could not be corrected
errors.title: Errors

TransformationNode.description: A transformation node in the ASG.

is_test_file.description: True if source is from a test file (path contains /tests/ or /test/)
is_test_file.title: Is Test File

is_empty_fallback.description: True if source is an empty DataFrame fallback (spark.createDataFrame([]))
is_empty_fallback.title: Is Empty Fallback

is_deterministic.description: False if transformation uses non-deterministic functions like current_timestamp(), rand(), uuid(). Affects Dynamic Table refresh strategy.
is_deterministic.title: Is Deterministic

non_deterministic_reason.description: Explanation of why this node is non-deterministic, e.g., 'uses current_timestamp()'
non_deterministic_reason.title: Non Deterministic Reason

is_convergence_point.description: True if this is the first node after control flow branches merge. Phase 2 uses convergence_inputs to build UNION/CASE logic.
is_convergence_point.title: Is Convergence Point

convergence_inputs.description: SSA-compliant IDs of branch outputs converging here, e.g., ['tx_015_TRUE', 'tx_018_FALSE'].
convergence_inputs.title: Convergence Inputs

TransformationNode.title: TransformationNode

TypeInferenceWarning.description: Warning when type inference falls back to name matching or excludes a call.
TypeInferenceWarning.title: TypeInferenceWarning

ok.description: Files where pipeline was successfully understood
ok.title: Ok

widget_type.description: Widget type: text, dropdown, combobox, multiselect
widget_type.title: Widget Type

default_value.description: Default value (literal or runtime:<expr> if dynamic)
default_value.title: Default Value

WindowSpecDefinition.description:
Definition of a PySpark Window specification.

Captures window specs assigned to variables (e.g., window = Window.partitionBy(...))
so they can be resolved during SQL generation.

scope.description: Function/scope where the window spec is defined
scope.title: Scope

WindowSpecDefinition.title: WindowSpecDefinition

description:
Abstract Semantic Graph (ASG) — the output of the parsing phase.

Contains the full structure of the analyzed Spark workload (PySpark, Scala, or any future language) with resolved references and propagated schemas.

Structure:
- workload_root: Absolute path to the workload directory (base for relative paths)
- source_files: List of SourceFile objects with per-file imports
- functions: Global list of functions, each with source_file field
- source_file: Path to the input (file or directory) - deprecated, use workload_root

control_nodes.description: Control flow structures extracted from Python AST
control_nodes.title: Control Nodes

parsing_report.description: Report of parsing results including file types and errors

column_constraints.description: Constraints on columns from filter conditions (for synthetic data)
column_constraints.title: Column Constraints

column_relationships.description: Relationships from join conditions (for synthetic data)
column_relationships.title: Column Relationships

title: ASG
